original: Greek archaeologist Valerios Stais discovers the Antikythera mechanism, an ancient mechanical analog computer
Phillies triple play Houston Colt .45s
Video game Minecraft is first released to the public while still in development
Léon Marchand, French swimmer (Olympic gold 200m breaststroke, 200m butterfly, 200m/400m IM 2024; WR 400m IM 4:02.50 2023), born in Toulouse, France
European Space Research Org launches 1st satellite
Les Anderson, catches record 97 lb 4 oz Chinook salmon, while fishing off coast of Alaska
New York Yankees pitcher David Wells tosses a perfect game in a 4-0 win against the Minnesota Twins at Yankee Stadium, NY
The first successful submarine that can run submerged for any considerable distance and combines electric and gasoline engines is launched in the USA by its designerJohn Philip Holland
David Cope, American composer known his pioneering work using artificial intelligence to create music, born in San Francisco, California (d. 2025)[1]
First atomic reactor system patented by J. W. Flora of Canoga Park, California
MLB Philadelphia Phillies win over Cubs, 23-22 in 10 innings at Wrigley Field on a breezy day in Chicago; teams combine for 13 first inning runs, and finish game with 50 hits, including 11 homeruns
Stanley Cup Final, Nassau Coliseum, Uniondale, NY: 4 consecutive titles for NY Islanders; sweep Edmonton Oilers in 4 games with a 4-2 Game 4 win
"Chicken Song" by Spitting Image hits #1 on the UK pop chart
Howard Ashman, American playwright and lyricist (Little Shop of Horrors: The Little Mermaid), born in Baltimore, Maryland (d. 1991)
The United States Army contracts with the University of Pennsylvania's Moore School to develop the ENIAC (Electronic Numerical Integrator And Computer).
US Supreme Court unanimously rules on Brown v Topeka Board of Education reverses 1896 "separate but equal" Plessy v Ferguson decision ruling racial segregation in public schools as illegal
Archaeologists announce the discovery of the oldest tomb in Mesoamerica at 2,700 years old, at Chiapa de Corzo, Mexico, containing the remains of a 50-year-old man
"The Wonderful Wizard of Oz" is first published byL. Frank Baumwith illustrations by William Wallace Denslow in Chicago
Nolan Bushnellopens the 1st Chuck E. Cheese's Pizza Time Theatre restaurant in  San Jose, California
Frederick McKinley Jones, American inventor (developments in refrigeration), born in Cincinnati, Ohio (d. 1961)
Russian probe Venera 6 landed on Venus
Italian Jesuit Niccolo Zucchi, 1st to see 2 belts on Jupiter surface
School desegregation law, Brown v Board of education
Coldest temperature ever recorded in Hawaii: 12°F (-11°C), on the Big Island at Mauna Kea Observatory, elevation 13,796 feet
Longest cab ride at 14,000 miles costs $16,000 (later surpassed)
Lydia Wideman, Finnish cross country skier (first female cross-country Olympic medalist, gold 10k 1952), born in Vippula, Finland (d. 2019)
Gary Paulsen, American author of young adult literature, born in Minneapolis, Minnesota (d. 2021)
Ferenc Krausz, Hungarian-Austrian physicist (2023 Nobel prize for Physics for attosecond pulses of light), born in Mór, Hungary[1]
The first color photograph, of a tartan ribbon is shown by Scottish scientistJames Clerk Maxwellto the Royal Institution in London
Comic Cuts, 1st weekly comic paper, published in London
World War II: the Dambuster Raids by No. 617 Squadron RAF on German dams
Stephen Schwartz' musical "Godspell" premieres off-Broadway
Aircraft carrier USS Oriskany is sunk in the Gulf of Mexico to form an artificial reef
Anonymous buyer purchases one of the pairs of ruby slippers worn byJudy Garlandin "The Wizard of Oz" for $15,000 at MGM Studio auction, and donates them to the Smithsonian Institution
Stevie Wonderreleases the music single "You Are The Sunshine Of My Life", goes to #1 and wins him a Grammy Award for Best Male Pop Vocal Performance
US GeneralDwight Eisenhowersets D-Day for June 5
Martinus Niewindt, Dutch Bishop of Curacao (advocate for fair treatment and abolition of slavery), born in Amsterdam (d. 1860)
Odd Hassel, Norwegian chemist (Nobel 1969 - the study of the three-dimensional geometric structure of molecules), born in Kristiana, Norway (d. 1981)
Antonio Aguilar [Barraza], Mexican singer, producer and actor (Emiliano Zapata) who popularized the Mexican equestrian sport la charrería, born in Villanueva, Zacatecas, Mexico (d. 2007)
John Davies, Australian swimmer (Olympic gold 200m breaststroke 1952; WR 200m breaststroke 2:34.4 1952), born in Sydney, Australia (d. 2020)
Bhagwat Chandrasekhar, Indian cricketer (brilliant Indian leggie 1964-79), born in Mysore, Karnataka
Jonti Picking [Jonathan Picking], British cartoonist (Weebl and Bob, Magical Trevor), born in Doncaster, England[1][2]
Angels outfielder Bobby Valentine breaks his leg trying to scale wall to prevent a Dick Green home run during a 5-4 loss to the A's
Giovanna Trillini, Italian fencer (2 x Olympic gold foil 1992), born in Iesi, Italy
Frenchmen Louis Jolliet and Jacques Marquette, a Jesuit, set out  from the Saint-Ignace mission on an expedition to explore the Mississippi river (reach border of Louisiana and Arkansas)
Erik Satie, French composer (Gymnopédies), born in Honfleur, Normandy (d. 1925)
Ilona Elek, Hungarian fencer (Olympic gold 1936, 48), born in Budapest, Hungary (d. 1988)
Jordan Knight, American singer-songwriter (New Kids on the Block), born in Worcester, Massachusetts
Cheick Kongo, French mixed martial artist and kickboxer (31 MMA wins, 21 kickboxing wins), born in Paris, France[1]
Tony Parker, French basketball guard (NBA C'ship 2003, 05, 07, 14; NBA Finals MVP 2007; 6 × NBA All-Star; San Antonio Spurs), born in Bruges, Belgium
The Bank of China Tower in Hong Kong designed byI. M. Peiopens as the first supertall skyscraper outside the US
7th recorded perihelion passage of Halley's Comet
First merry-go-round is seen at a fair in Philippopolis in the Ottoman Empire
John Hawkins and Richard French patent the reaping machine
Australian Rules Football first 'laws of the game' published
Alaska becomes a US territory
Victoria & Albert Museum foundation laid, London, England
MLB Chicago Cubs beat Boston Braves, 4-3, in 22 innings, at Braves Field in Boston, Massachusetts
Operation Straightline: Allies land in Dutch New Guinea
British government recognises Republic of Ireland (previously Irish Free State)
